title: Workhorse Group Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Results
text:
Workhorse Group Reports Fourth Quarter and Full Year 2023 Results

Publication Date: March 12, 2024

Key Financial Highlights

For full year 2023, Workhorse generated $13.1 million in sales, up from $5.0 million in 2022. However, the company posted a net loss of $123.9 million compared to $117.3 million in the prior year.

Fourth quarter 2023 sales reached $4.4 million versus $3.4 million year-over-year. The company reported a Q4 net loss of $45.3 million, compared to $38.7 million in Q4 2022.

As of December 31, 2023, Workhorse held $25.8 million in cash and equivalents, plus $10 million in restricted cash.

Strategic Developments

CEO Rick Dauch highlighted progress on the product roadmap: we rolled out our first W56 step van, secured our first W56 fleet customers, and expanded dealer networks across multiple states.

The company secured final HVIP certification for the W56 step van in Q4 2023 and received its first two 15-vehicle fleet orders. Production of the W4 CC and W750 models has stabilized.

Workhorse completed manufacturing facility upgrades at its Union City, Indiana location, with capacity for over 5,000 vehicles annually. The company entered a non-binding sale-leaseback agreement for this facility in Q1 2024.

Financial Restructuring

To strengthen its financial position, Workhorse is: Negotiating financing arrangements for liquidity improvements. Executing an approximate 20% workforce reduction. Implementing 20% executive compensation deferrals. Transitioning its Aero division from manufacturing to a Drones-as-a-Service model.
